title: Podman-apparmor issues on Ubuntu 24.04
body:
### Issue Description

Apparmor: cannot set complain mode for podman

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. new Ubuntu 24.04 machine
apparmor is enabled:
```
systemctl is-active apparmor
active
```
podman is not installed:
```
dpkg -l podman
dpkg-query: no packages found matching podman
```
Podman is under enforcing mode in `aa-status`.
available podman version:
```
apt-cache show podman | head -n3
Package: podman
Architecture: amd64
Version: 4.9.3+ds1-1ubuntu0.2
```
2. install podman:
```
apt-get install podman -y
```
3. try to set complain mode:
```
aa-complain podman

ERROR: Operation {'runbindable'} cannot have a source. Source = AARE('/')
```


### Describe the results you received

see error above

### Describe the results you expected

podman should be set to complain mode

### podman info output
```
podman info
podman: error while loading shared libraries: libsubid.so.4: cannot open shared object file: Permission denied
```

Version 4.9.3+ds1-1ubuntu0.2 on Ubuntu 24.04, amd64.


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

both

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Upstream only supports the latest version. Also we have no apparmor expertise on the team and do not ship an apparmor profile for podman so this is not an upstream issue. You need to report this to your distro
